entry.title: Re: Using Migration Assessment to Plan Your SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite Migra
entry.content: @Shashi  Big migration projects are very sensitive to time and Normal migration strategy doesnt work with them. For example for migrating 1000 integrations on SAP PO I would need atleast 2600 - 3000 days for a single resource (less for multiple resources) , no customer would agree to this. I think this is where pipeline helps. Pipeline concept will make your migrations faster but it goes against the principle that migration is an opportunity to soft upgrade like  simplify, make the integrations more robust, redesign some with API's etc. So to answer your question I think use pipeline concept for large customers IF they are not happy with the effort estimates you provide and call these estimates too large. SAP's migration tool is getting better day by day and that's what I have used for years as whatever tool you use you need to spend "SAME" effort in cleaning the integration up or for the finishing touch. BUT for large customers do use INT4's regression testing tool it can bring down testing effort by 90%

entry.title: Re: Need to add PO to Invoice, but didn't find the place...
entry.content:
Hello customer,

If it's not possible for you to add the missing PO number to the invoice in the Finance & Legal dashboard in SAP for Me, then very possible the corresponding contract is currently not eligible for PO submission via SAP for Me.

For additional support in submitting the PO number, please use the 'Get assistance' option available under the 'Manage Contract' menu on the last column of the Contracts table.

The 'Get assistance' button is also available under the Billing tab. Select the invoice check-box first, then the button is clickable. 

The submitted form will reach the PO Collection team directly and they will be able to assist further.

This is documented in KBA 3707175 - Manage PO Numbers button is not clickable - SAP for Me, kindly refer to the KBA for more details.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation Not Permitted for Vendor or Customer in Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content:
When executing Foreign Currency Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system raises an error stating that Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation is not permitted for the selected valuation area when Vendor or Customer open items are included in the run.

The Year-End and Mid-Year indicators are visible in the job parameters. However, activating them together with Vendor or Customer valuation results in an error. The business requirement is to perform year-end foreign currency valuation for open items without an automatic reversal in the following fiscal period.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation Not Permitted for Vend...
entry.content:
This is standard system behavior.

Foreign Currency Valuation differentiates between open item valuation and balance valuation. Vendor and Customer accounts are open item managed accounts. For these accounts, valuation postings represent unrealized exchange rate differences. These postings are automatically reset by an inverse posting in the subsequent fiscal period. This ensures that the full realized exchange rate difference is correctly determined at clearing.

The Year-End and Mid-Year Valuation indicators require delta logic to be active for the valuation area. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, delta logic is not generally available and is activated by SAP only for specific countries based on legal requirements. If delta logic is not active for the valuation area, the system prevents Vendor or Customer valuation when these indicators are selected.

To perform year-end valuation for Vendor or Customer open items, execute the standard Foreign Currency Valuation run as of the required key date without selecting the Year-End or Mid-Year indicator. The resulting postings will impact the financial statements of the selected fiscal year. The automatic reversal in the subsequent fiscal period does not affect the closed fiscal year reporting.

If non-reversing postings are required for specific business reasons, this must be handled outside of standard Foreign Currency Valuation, for example through manual journal entries.

entry.title: Unlock the Power of People Management with Joule
entry.content:
In the modern workplace, managers face growing challenges in fostering team development, improving performance, and identifying future leaders. SAP’s Joule introduces the People Manager Assistant—a role-specific AI tool designed to empower managers to efficiently support their teams.

The People Manager Assistant goes beyond typical management tools. It uses intelligent analysis of employee data, feedback, and performance reviews to pinpoint areas for improvement. By providing proactive insights on development goals, personalized growth plans, and succession planning timelines, this assistant helps managers streamline the talent development process. Imagine being able to foster employee growth with precision while saving valuable time to focus on strategic leadership.

Whether creating clear development roadmaps or analyzing workforce performance, the People Manager Assistant turns complex people-related decisions into actionable intelligence. It's not just about managing teams—it's about empowering them. With Joule managers can inspire growth, prioritize team alignment, and drive performance like never before.

Let’s look at an example of an AI Assistant in action:

Disclaimer: The names used in the following story are fictional, and any resemblance to actual persons is purely coincidental.

Christine’s potential needed direction, so her manager, Alex, tapped the People Manager Assistant for insights. The assistant identified key growth areas and crafted actionable goals. Later, Tara was assessed for succession, revealing promise with development needs. In minutes, structured plans were created, ensuring growth and leadership continuity for the organization.

 

Summary:

• Joule empowers faster decision-making and streamlines workflows using harmonized data and trusted architecture.
• Role-specific AI assistants offer tailored support to handle complex tasks efficiently in various business functions.
• The People Manager Assistant analyzes employee data to develop growth plans, identify successors, and enhance team performance.
• Joule Agents deliver measurable improvements, optimizing productivity across areas like HR Operations and Accounts Receivable Management.

entry.title: Benefits of Maintenance Planning Overview Fiori Application
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of industrial asset management, the difference between a high-performing plant and a costly bottleneck often comes down to how quickly a maintenance planner can identify and react to issues.

In the legacy SAP environment, this meant digging through multiple transaction codes (T-codes) to find overdue orders or missing parts. With SAP S/4HANA, the Maintenance Planning Overview (App ID: F2828) Fiori application changes the game by consolidating critical data into a single, proactive dashboard.

Here is an overview of why this app is becoming the "command center" for modern maintenance planners.

What is the Maintenance Planning Overview App?

The Maintenance Planning Overview is an analytical Fiori application designed as a "one-stop shop" for Maintenance Planners. Built on the SAP Fiori Overview Page (OVP) floorplan, it uses interactive "cards" to display high-level KPIs and real-time data visualizations.

Instead of searching for problems, the problems are pushed to you. The app analyzes factors like open notifications, missing spare parts, and overdue orders based on a reference period (e.g., "This Week" or "Next Week") that you define.

Key Features: Your Planning Toolkit

The app is organized into task-oriented cards. Each card serves a specific purpose in the maintenance lifecycle:

1. Planning & Backlog Management

  • Orders for Planning: Identifies maintenance orders that have been created but not yet released. It visualizes these by priority or order type using pie charts.

  • Outstanding Notifications: Shows notifications that haven't been assigned to an order yet, allowing you to catch work requests before they fall through the cracks.

2. Material & Procurement Visibility

  • Purchase Requisitions/Orders Not Approved: Tracks non-stock materials required for maintenance. You can see which parts might cause delays because the requisition hasn't been approved or the purchase order hasn't been sent.

  • Non-Stock Materials Not Available: A critical card that flags ordered parts that will not arrive by the required start date of the maintenance order.

3. Execution Tracking

  • Overdue Orders: Highlights released orders where the end date has passed but the operations haven't been finally confirmed.

  • Orders Not Technically Completed: Provides a list of orders that are physically finished but haven't been closed out in the system, which is vital for accurate financial reporting.

 

 

The app typically includes the following standard Fiori cards:

1. Maintenance Order Cards

  • Orders for Planning: Visualizes maintenance orders that have been created but not yet released. It usually breaks them down by Priority or Order Type.

  • Overdue Orders: Analyzes released maintenance orders where the basic end date has already passed, but the operations have not been finally confirmed.

  • Orders for Completion: Specifically highlights orders where all operations are confirmed (CNF) but the order header has not yet been set to Technically Completed (TECO).

  • Jobs (Order Operations): Displays a list of upcoming or current maintenance "jobs" (operations) that require attention or execution within the defined reference period.

2. Maintenance Notification Cards

  • Outstanding Notifications: Displays notifications that have been created but are not yet assigned to a maintenance order. This helps prevent a backlog of unaddressed work requests.

  • Recently Created Notifications: Gives a quick look at the newest requests entering the system, categorized by their processing status.

3. Procurement & Material Cards

  • Non-Stock Materials Not Available: This is a high-value card that flags non-stock components (parts ordered specifically for an order) that are not expected to arrive by the order's start date.

  • Purchase Requisitions Not Approved: Identifies PRs linked to maintenance orders that are stalled in the approval workflow, which could delay planned repairs.

  • Purchase Requisitions Not Converted: Shows approved requisitions that have not yet been turned into formal Purchase Orders (POs).

4. Technical Object Cards

  • Technical Object Damages: Often used to visualize which assets are experiencing the most frequent issues, categorized by damage codes or object parts.

     

Key Features of These Cards

  • Filter Sensitivity: All cards react to the global filters at the top of the app (e.g., Planning Plant, Planner Group, or Time Period).

  • Drill-Down Capability: Clicking the header of a card takes you to a detailed list view (like the "Manage Maintenance Orders" app), while clicking a specific data point (like a slice of a pie chart) filters that list further.

  • Personalization: Users can hide cards they don't need or rearrange them on the dashboard to put the most critical KPIs at the top

entry.title: HANA Client Version Impact on HANABulkCopy Performance
entry.content:
I am using SAP Business One 10.0 for SAP HANA FP 2505 with the following environment:

  • SAP HANA 2.0 Enterprise Edition SPS 05 Revision 2.00.059.09.1686579463 (fa/hana2sp05)
  • SAP HANA Client version 2.17.22
  • SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP5

I am encountering an unexpected performance issue when using HANABulkCopy from the SAP HANA ADO.NET library to copy data from a .NET DataTable into an SAP HANA column table.

Issue

With SAP HANA Client 2.17.22, running HANABulkCopy takes 30–40 minutes, even when inserting only 2 rows, and sometimes the operation times out.

What I tried

I installed a newer SAP HANA Client version 2.27.19, and with the exact same code and table:

  • Inserting 2 rows or even 20,000 rows now takes only 1–2 seconds.
  • No timeouts occur.
  • Performance is consistently stable.

My question

The SAP Business One Administrator’s Guide for SAP HANA recommends using SAP HANA Client version 2.17.22 for this B1 version.

So I am trying to understand:

  • Why does version 2.17.22 cause such extreme delays with HANABulkCopy?
  • Why does upgrading to 2.27.19 immediately resolve the issue, even though it is not the version officially recommended for SAP Business One?

Has anyone else experienced this behavior with HANABulkCopy on older HANA Client versions?
Is there any known limitation, bug, or compatibility issue with 2.17.22 that would explain this performance drop?

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Publish events from CAP Node.js applications to Event Mesh of Integration Suite
entry.content:
In this blog I’ll show how to publish events from a CAP application to SAP Integration Suite’s Event Mesh. Both the CAP application and Event Mesh are located in the same BTP subaccount. I’ll skip the setup steps for Event Mesh and focus specifically on publishing events from the CAP application.

Step 1: start by creating a new CAP Node.js application in Business Application Studio:

cds init emis-samebtp
touch srv/service.cds
touch srv/service.js

Step 2: in service.cds, define a simple service with a function that triggers an event:

service GreetingService {
  function helloEvent(to: String) returns String;
}

In service.js, implement the function to emit an event to Event Mesh:

const eventTopic = 'cap/demo/v1/sample/hello-event';

module.exports = async (srv) => {
  const messaging = await cds.connect.to('messaging');

  srv.on('helloEvent', async (req) => {
    const target = req.data.to;
    const data = { To: target, Message: 'Hello world events!' };
    
    await messaging.emit(eventTopic, data);
    req.notify(`Event sent > ${eventTopic}`);
    
    return `Hello event: ${target}`;
  });
};

Step 3: add the necessary services for authentication and messaging:

cds add xsuaa --for-production
cds add enterprise-messaging-shared --for-production

Then update your package.json to configure the messaging service:

"cds": {
  "server": { "index": true },
  "requires": {
    "[production]": {
      "auth": "xsuaa",
      "messaging": {
        "kind": "enterprise-messaging-shared",
        "publishPrefix": "stc/emis/",
        "format": "cloudevents",
        "vcap": {
          "label": "event-mesh-message-client",
          "plan": "message-client",
          "name": "emis-samebtp-messaging"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Step 4: generate the MTA deployment descriptor:

npm install
cds build --production
cds add mta

Edit the generated mta.yaml to ensure the resource definitions match your package.json configuration.

Step 5: run the build and deploy commands to push your application to BTP. 

cds build
cf deploy mta_archives/your-archive.mtar

Step 6: Testing the Integration

Navigate to the Event Mesh section in SAP Integration Suite. Select a queue and subscribe to the topic "stc/emis/cap/demo/v1/sample/hello-event". 

Then use the OAuth client credentials from XSUAA to call your CAP service "https://<your-hostname>:443/odata/v4/greeting/helloEvent?to=Mark".

 If everything is configured correctly, you’ll receive a response and see the event appear in your Event Mesh queue.

This setup is ideal for scenarios where you need real-time eventing between BTP applications and other SAP or non-SAP systems. Give it a try and let me know how it goes in the comments!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning with Live Snowflake Data
entry.content:
Great update!, thanks for sharing the QRC1 2026 extension to live planning sources. The walkthrough of particularly interesting to see how live Snowflake connectivity further expands SAC’s ability to leverage external cloud data platforms. I would be interested in understanding how this approach performs with large Snowflake datasets (e.g., >100M rows), especially in terms of query latency, pushdown optimization, and overall planning responsiveness.

Additionally, are there recommended best practices for handling complex hierarchies, such as time or custom business hierarchies, within Snowflake views to ensure stable and efficient planning calculations in SAC?

Lastly, is there a reference list of Snowflake data types that are currently unsupported for live versions? Having this visibility would help teams validate view design upfront and avoid modelling constraints during implementation. Creating a planning model directly from a Snowflake view and keeping Actuals live while writing Plans into SAC is a very pragmatic and scalable design pattern, especially for organizations aiming to min.

entry.title: Shadow AI対策：SAP AI Core で実現する安全なAI活用環境
entry.content:
1. 仕組み：SAPが仲介し、認証情報を隠蔽する

個人でChatGPTを使う場合、PCから直接OpenAI社のサーバーにデータが送られます。一方、SAPの環境では、SAP AI Core や Generative AI Hub が「仲介役（プロキシ）」として動作します。アプリケーションは、SAP BTP内のプライベートなエンドポイント（外部インターネットから直接アクセスできない、SAP BTPで管理された安全な接続口）へアクセスするため、直接外部のAIサービスと通信することはありません。

さらに、SAP BTPの Connectivity Service を利用すれば、接続先URLや認証情報（Client ID/Secretなど）を一元管理できます。これにより、アプリケーションのコードの中に機密情報を書く必要がなくなり、開発者はより安全に大規模言語モデル（LLM）を利用することができます。Connectivity Serviceの詳細は次のURLを参照してください。https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/connectivity?locale=en-US

2. 技術的詳細：入力データを自動で守る「Orchestration」

SAP AI Core には「Orchestration（オーケストレーション）」という機能があります。これは、大規模言語モデル（LLM）への問い合わせに対して、送信前の入力チェックや送信後の出力フィルタリングなど、複数の処理を統合的に実行できる機能です。

Shadow AI対策として特に重要なのが以下の2点です。

  • 個人情報のマスキング: 従業員が誤って個人情報（メールアドレスや氏名など）を入力した場合でも、Orchestration機能がAIへの送信直前に自動的に検知し、伏せ字（マスキング）に変換します。これにより、外部モデルへの個人情報流出を未然に防ぎます。
  • 有害な入力のブロック: 差別的な発言や暴力的な内容が含まれていないかをチェックし、企業のポリシーに反する入力を遮断します。

これらの具体的な設定方法（JSONパラメータの記述例など）については、私が執筆した以下のブログ記事で詳しく解説しています。実装を検討される方は、ぜひこちらをご参照ください。

  • SAP AI CoreにおけるOrchestration機能とセキュリティ設定の実装ガイド

3. プロンプトのライフサイクル管理：Prompt Registry

Shadow AIのもう一つの問題は、「誰がどんなプロンプトを使っているかわからず、品質や結果が一貫しない」ことです。これを解決するのが Prompt Registry です。

これは、プロンプトを単なるテキストではなく「バージョン管理されたライブラリ」として中央で管理するサービスです。設計から実行（runtime）までのライフサイクル全体を管理できるため、アプリケーション間での一貫性と再利用性が保証されます。 コンセプトについては SAP Learning: Managing Prompts with the Prompt Registry and Templates でも詳しく解説されています。

技術的には、以下の2つの方法でプロンプトを管理・利用できます。

  1. Declarative API（Git連携）: Gitリポジトリ上のプロンプト定義（repo_name を指定）と自動的に同期し、コードの一部として管理する方法。
  2. Imperative API（直接操作）: API経由でテンプレートの ID や、名前・シナリオ・バージョンの組み合わせを指定して取得・更新する方法。

これにより、従業員が勝手なプロンプトを使うのではなく、会社が承認し、バージョン管理された安全なプロンプト（Approved Prompts）だけを利用するよう統制を効かせることが可能になります。 開発者向けの実装ガイドは SAP Cloud SDK for AI: Prompt Registry が参考になります。

4. まとめ

Shadow AIを防ぐには、ルールで縛るだけでなく、技術的な解決策が必要です。

SAP AI Core / Generative AI Hub を仲介役として利用し、Destination Service で認証情報を管理することで、安全な接続経路を確立できます。さらに、Orchestration によるデータの保護や Prompt Registry による利用管理を組み合わせることで、企業は安心してAI活用を進めることができます。

entry.title: Trigger Custom API on Create & Save of Warehouse Task in /SCWM/POST (EWM)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement in SAP EWM where, upon clicking Create + Save while creating a Warehouse Task in transaction /SCWM/POST, a custom API should be triggered automatically.

I attempted to implement this using BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST, but it did not meet the requirement and the API is not getting triggered as expected.

Since I am relatively new to the EWM module, I would appreciate guidance on:

  • The correct enhancement spot / BAdI to use for triggering logic after Warehouse Task creation and save

  • Whether there is a Post-Commit or Save-related BAdI specifically for Warehouse Task processing

  • Any recommended approach (Enhancement Spot, BAdI, User Exit, or implicit enhancement) to achieve this requirement

The goal is to ensure the API is called only after the Warehouse Task is successfully created and saved.

Any suggestions or references would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Story Versioning in SAP Analytics Cloud: Design with Confidence & Iterate Without Fear!
entry.content:
Designing impactful stories in SAP Analytics Cloud is rarely a straight line. Instead, Story Designers iterate constantly by experimenting with layouts, refining narratives, adjusting calculations, and incorporating stakeholder feedback. Until now, that iterative process often came with a trade-off: move fast, or play it safe.

Without a native way to manage major story iterations, designers relied on manual copies, naming conventions like “Corporate_Overview_Final_v7_REAL_FINAL,” or external documentation to track changes. This made it harder to experiment confidently, collaborate transparently, and recover from changes that didn’t land as expected.

With the 2026 Q1 QRC, we’re introducing Story Versioning. A foundational capability that gives Story Designers the freedom to iterate, experiment, and refine their work, while staying fully in control.

Story Versioning

Story Versioning is designed to be simple by default, while still supporting real-world design workflows. There’s no separate tool or workflow to learn as it is embedded directly into the existing authoring experience, allowing designers to adopt it naturally without disrupting how they already work. Let's look into a few of the core capabilities. 

Explicit Major Versions with Designer Control

Story Designers can create up to 10 versions per story, each acting as a major version. Creating a new version is an intentional action and are never created automatically in the background.

Once the maximum of ten versions is reached, designers choose which existing version to delete before creating a new one. This approach ensures flexibility, allowing designers to retain the versions that matter most rather than assuming the oldest version is no longer relevant.

Each version can also have a descriptive note, making it easy to capture context such as review milestones, experiments, or key design changes.

View, Restore, or Save Versions Safely

Any previous version can be opened in a read-only view, allowing designers to review earlier iterations without impacting the current version.

From there, designers can either save the version as a new story or restore it. When a version is restored, SAP Analytics Cloud automatically creates a backup of the current version, ensuring that all changes remain reversible and no work is lost unintentionally.

Always Aligned with the Latest Dependent Objects

Stories often consume multiple dependent objects such as models, themes, composites, and so on, each with its own lifecycle. Story Versioning always consumes the latest state of these dependent objects.

Predictable Transport Behavior

When transporting a story using the Content Network (ACN), only the current active version is included. This keeps transports clean and predictable, while aligning with existing lifecycle and promotion workflows.

Version Visibility in the File Repository

All created versions are visible through the file repository, giving designers transparency into a story’s evolution directly from content management views.

 

Why Story Versioning Matters

Story Versioning fundamentally changes how Story Designers work in SAP Analytics Cloud.

It enables confident experimentation without fear of breaking a story, clearer tracking across major design milestones, safer collaboration when incorporating feedback, and simpler recovery from changes that don’t pan out.

Most importantly, it shifts story design from a cautious, copy-heavy workflow to one that encourages creative exploration with guardrails. This capability also lays the foundation for future versioning for other objects in SAP Analytics Cloud, allowing all personas to focus less on managing risk and more on completing their tasks.

entry.title: Working with SAP Kapsel SDK and older Android devices with DigiCert G5 Certificate
entry.content:
Background

SAP is changing the root certificate of the domains BTP to DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 CA, see SAP Note 3566727 for more details.

The new certificate is supported by Android 14 and later. For older devices that cannot be upgraded, the certificate has to be installed on the device or embedded in the Android App. This post explains the application embeding approach, for Mobile Applications built using the Kapsel SDK. 

Important: The following approach essentially adds the DigiCert G5 certificate such that the application can validate the SAP Certificate signed by it. As such, great care should be taken to ensure that correct certificate is being added. The DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 used in the following was downloaded from DigiCert Trusted Root Authority Certificates.

1. Download DigiCert G5 certificate

1. Download the root certificate from https://cacerts.digicert.com/DigiCertTLSRSA4096RootG5.crt mentioned in the above note

2. Rename the file to all lowercase letters.  I have used the name digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5.crt for readability

2. Online Functionality Only

1. In Kapsel Application project root path create the following new subdirectories if they don’t already exist: res/android/raw, res/android/xml.

2. Copy the certificate file to res/android/raw, creating res/android/raw/digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5.crt

3. Create file res/android/xml/kapsel_network_security_config.xml and add a reference to the certificate file. In the following example, the App trusts servers that identify with this certificate in addition to all other certificates in the System store.

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='utf-8'?>
<network-security-config>
    <base-config cleartextTrafficPermitted="true">
        <trust-anchors>
            <certificates src="system" />
            <certificates src="user" />
            <certificates src="@raw/digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5digicert_root_g5_crt" />
        </trust-anchors>
    </base-config>
</network-security-config>

3. Edit config.xml file and add following two properties under the `<platform name="android">` elements:

    <resource-file src="res/android/raw/digicert_root_g5_crt" target="app/src/main/res/raw/digicert_root_g5_crt" />
    <resource-file src="res/android/xml/kapsel_network_security_config.xml" target="app/src/main/res/xml/kapsel_network_security_config.xml" />

3. Offline Functionality

In addition to above, the following changes are required for apps utilizing the Offline functionality, providing the certificate as a ‘trusted_certificates’ in ‘streamParams’ property used for creating Offline Store. 

1. Create new subdirectory if it doesn't already exist: www/certs.

2. Copy the certificate file to www/certs.

3. Code modification

Locate the code where `createOfflineStore` is called and add `extraStreamParameters` as follows. Ensure that the property is not already assigned, which would overwrite these changes. In that case, concatenate this with existing value.  Note that this is based on an App created with the Sample Service feature with two defining requests, please adjust for your application.

 

        function openStore() {
            console.log("EventLogging: openStore");
            
            ensureTrustedRootsFile(function (certFileURL) {
                startTime = new Date();
                updateStatus2("store.open called");
                var properties = {
                        "enableIndividualErrorArchiveDeletion": true,
                        "name": "ESPMOfflineStore",
                        "host": applicationContext.registrationContext.serverHost,
                        "port": applicationContext.registrationContext.serverPort,
                        "https": applicationContext.registrationContext.https,
                        "serviceRoot": "sampleodatav4",
                        "definingRequests": {
                            "SuppliersDR": "/Suppliers",
                            "CategoriesDR": "/Categories"
                        },
                        "streamParams": "trusted_certificates=" + certFileURL + ";"
                    };
                if (certFileURL != null) {
                    properties.streamParams = "trusted_certificates=" + certFileURL + ";";
                }                 
                store = sap.OData.createOfflineStore(properties);
                store.onrequesterror = onRequestError; //called for each modification error during flush
                var options = {};
                store.open(openStoreSuccessCallback, errorCallback, options, progressCallback);
            });
        }

        function ensureTrustedRootsFile(callback) {
            // The certificate file needs to be copied from the www directory to the data directory for the following reason:
            // The www directory is located inside the app sandbox and cannot be accessed directly by the offline odata module.
            // The data directory is a readable/writable area for the app, and the offline odata module can access files in the data directory.
            var certFileName = 'digicert_root_g5_crt';
            var srcPath = cordova.file.applicationDirectory + 'www/certs/' + certFileName;
            var destDir = cordova.file.dataDirectory;
            var destPath = destDir + certFileName;
            // Check if the target file already exists
            window.resolveLocalFileSystemURL(destPath, function (existingEntry) {
                // If the file exists, return its native absolute path
                var nativePath = existingEntry.nativeURL;
                if (nativePath.startsWith('file://')) {
                    nativePath = nativePath.substring(7);
                }
                if (!nativePath.startsWith('/')) {
                    nativePath = '/' + nativePath;
                }
                console.log('Certificate already exists at: ' + nativePath);
                callback(nativePath);
            }, function () {
                // If the file does not exist, perform the copy operation
                window.resolveLocalFileSystemURL(srcPath, function (srcEntry) {
                    window.resolveLocalFileSystemURL(destDir, function (destDirEntry) {
                        // Copy the certificate file to the data directory
                        srcEntry.copyTo(destDirEntry, certFileName, function (newFileEntry) {
                            var nativePath = newFileEntry.nativeURL;
                            if (nativePath.startsWith('file://')) {
                                nativePath = nativePath.substring(7);
                            }
                            if (!nativePath.startsWith('/')) {
                                nativePath = '/' + nativePath;
                            }
                            console.log('Certificate copied to: ' + nativePath);
                            callback(nativePath);
                        }, function (copyErr) {
                            alert('Failed to copy certificate file: ' + JSON.stringify(copyErr));
                            callback(null);
                        });
                    }, function (destErr) {
                        alert('Destination directory not found: ' + destDir);
                        callback(null);
                    });
                }, function (srcErr) {
                    alert('Certificate file not found: ' + srcPath + '. Please ensure the file is in the correct location.');
                    callback(null);
                });
            });
        }

Verification

  • Launch the Kapsel app and connect to your server.
  • If needed, use Logcat to confirm the TLS handshake succeeds (no CERTIFICATE_UNKNOWN or SSLHandshakeException).
  • Test on the target Android versions you plan to support.

Troubleshooting

  • Confirm you exported the root certificate: "DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5."
  • Verify the file is Base64 (.crt/.pem) and not a binary DER.

Conclusion

As explained above, without any changes the mobile app may not be able to connect after the switch to G5 and result in complete outage. Feel free to post any follow-up questions here.

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Build Apps Subscription Visibility under build-default Plan
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need your clarification regarding SAP Build Apps subscription visibility in our SAP BTP subaccount.

Currently, our subaccount has the following subscriptions active under the build-default plan:

SAP Build Process Automation – build-default

SAP Business Application Studio – build-default

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – build-default

As per SAP documentation and licensing understanding, SAP Build Apps is included within the build-default plan and does not appear as a standalone subscription.

However, when we attempt to create a project using Create Project → Web Application (low-code) in SAP Build Apps, the system displays the status as “Not Subscribed”, which creates confusion from an entitlement perspective.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether SAP Build Apps entitlement is correctly provided through the build-default plan in our subaccount, and

  2. Why the project creation wizard displays “Not Subscribed” despite the build-default plan being active.

Your confirmation will help us proceed and also communicate the correct information to our stakeholders.

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: Re: HANA Client Version Impact on HANABulkCopy Performance
entry.content:
Hello,

This is indeed a fixed issue. This is Issue Number 340385, fixed in SAP HANA Client 2.24.24. See the SAP HANA Client 2.24 release notes here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3566142

@ManishPant wrote:
  • Why does upgrading to 2.27.19 immediately resolve the issue, even though it is not the version officially recommended for SAP Business One?

It's not clear why SAP Business One recommends a specific SAP HANA Client version, other than this is likely the version that has been tested with your current version of SAP Business One. It is possible that newer versions of SAP Business One have tested later versions of SAP HANA Client.

From an SAP HANA Client and SAP HANA Server perspective, the client and server are both forwards and backwards compatible (see: https://me.sap.com/notes/1906576 for more details). In general, if you are encountering a problem with your specific version of SAP HANA Client we would recommend that you first try an SAP HANA Client upgrade to the latest available version in order to determine if your problem is fixed in a newer version (which is true in your specific case).

For more details about the SAP HANA Client maintenance policy, see: https://me.sap.com/notes/2769719

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP POs to Ariba B&I without Commerce Automation
entry.content:
Dear @velayudham,

Good day!

As per my understanding there are two potential options we have, to address the above requirement
  • Option 1: Leverage the existing Import Purchase Requisition Functionality to create a PR (Auto approve in Ariba)—> Auto Generation of PO happens —> PO Communicated to Network via Native Ariba integration—> PO available in Supplier Portal.
    • Import Methods:
      • Data Import Task (CSV): Using Core Administration → Site Manager → Import/Export → Data Import/Export task
      • Web Services: Real-time integration using SOAP/REST APIs with cXML format.
      • Excel Upload: Bulk import using Excel workbooks, including ZIP file support for multiple requisitions
    • The above option is more convenient and make sense only if customer is creating a Purchase Requisition Document in S/4 HANA before releasing the PO Directly. 
  • Option 2: Using the BTP Integration Suite -CPI Module 
    • If customer is not creating PR document in S/4 HANA and follows the PO creation approach directly for any sort of procurement need, then you can essentially use the Integration Suite- CPI to transform the Purchase Order created in S/4 HANA system to PR Import format in Ariba and push the transform document into Ariba via above mentioned web-service method. 
PS: In Ariba, based on the landscape considerations you outlined, the system does not provide a direct option to load Purchase Orders. However, a fully supported and effective alternative is available through the PR Import functionality, which remains the recommended approach for bringing POs into the system. The communication channel for this process can be selected from any of the options mentioned above
 
Hope this helps. 
 
Regards,
Ajay Kumar

entry.title: Re: Review Order Progress - Multilevel View
entry.content:
Good day @Moon3 

The behaviour you described is usually caused by planning data differences between systems (tenants) not by the app itself.

The PR to PO relation in Review Order Progress- Multilevel View is based on supply and demand elements. If one system (tenant) updates only after the next MRP run, it often means the planning situation is only recalculated during MRP in that tenant. The app itself was introduced and enhanced in recent releases but it does not replace the initial planning logic

The following blogs provide some good insights: DEMOs: App Review Order Progress - Multilevel View and

Manufacturing in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

You can compare both tenant environments by checking your MRP relevant master data, MRP run setup and execution timing, Procurement proposal conversion behaviour and your App filters and object selection consistency. This should show you why customising tenant shows immediate PR to PO change, while your test tenant requires another MRP initiation.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Programmatically retrieving lineage/source object info for Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to understand whether it is possible to programmatically retrieve lineage metadata for an Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere.

Use case:
Within the Datasphere UI, the Impact & Lineage view allows us to trace a field from an Analytical Model back to its underlying objects (views and source tables). I am currently developing a small web application intended to List AMs automatically along with few technical details(for example: fields, and their originating tables/views).

Using the Consumption OData API, I am already able to extract the list of fields of an Analytical Model:

GET https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/datasphere/consumption/analytical/<space_id>/<asset_id>/$metadata

This provides the semantic fields exposed by the model.


However, my requirement is to enrich this information with the technical source information, specifically:

Analytical Model Field → Datasphere View Column → Underlying Table (or Remote Table)

Example:

I would like to know:

  1. Is there a REST API available to retrieve lineage information similar to what is shown in the Datasphere Impact & Lineage diagram?

  2. If not, are there any supported system metadata tables/views (for example via SQL access to the space) that can be used to obtain this mapping?

Any guidance or recommended approach would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

SAP Datasphere API

entry.title: Re: how to reverse the client copy
entry.content:
Hello,

client copy deletes in first place all data in target client. So if you want to bring the data back, you probably have to do a restore for your system. there is no turning back feature in client copy. another thing would be that you do a client export of your target client in first place, so you can use this client export for restore and do not need to restore whole system.

also you can check out the copy profiles

https://help.sap.com/saphelp_snc700_ehp04/helpdata/en/4d/806794095114afe10000000a42189c/content.htm?no_cache=true

 

 

br

entry.title: Re: SmartForms: One Delivery Order with Multiple Customer...
entry.content:
Yes, what you’re seeing is standard SAP behavior. In SD billing, invoice splitting is controlled by split criteria. Even if the delivery is the same, SAP checks certain header fields when creating the billing document. One of those fields is the Customer PO number (VBKD-BSTKD). If items in the delivery reference different PO numbers, SAP treats them as requiring separate billing documents.
Check VTFL where you have two fields "Assignment" and "Reference" where it should not be "A" in which case, you will get invoice split. Also, you might be aware, the cause of the billing split is due to VBRK-ZUKRI. Check what value is there. Finally, implement custom validation to restrict this in such a way to generate one billing document against one delivery.  Once this is acheived, your Smartform issue will also be taken care automatically.

entry.title: Course Wrap-Up and Forum Update
entry.content:
Dear course participants, 

We hope you enjoyed the SAP Expert Lecture “Designing Agentic Systems with a Human-Centered Approach” and found it valuable for your professional development. 

As of today, the course discussion forum is no longer actively moderated.  

Thank you to everyone who took the time to share questions, ideas and feedback during the course period — your engagement made the learning experience richer for all. 

If you’d still like to share feedback, the following options remain available: 

  • Use the smileys below each lesson video to provide feedback on specific content. 
  • For technical or general questions, use the Feedback function in the top left corner of the SAP Learning page. 

We look forward to seeing you in a future SAP Expert Lecture. 

Thank you for choosing SAP Learning! 

Kind regards, 
Your SAP Expert Lecture Team 

(SAP Learning)

entry.title: Re: Secure Login Service Initial Authentication - Move aw...
entry.content:
Hi Kal,

 what you describe (Kerberos/SPNEGO → “virtual user” on Secure Login Server → short-lived X.509 → SNC SSO to ABAP) is exactly the kind of scenario that SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI is meant to solve when you want to move away from on-prem AD/KDC and still keep certificate-based SNC SSO for SAP GUI. 

Key point: With Secure Login Service for SAP GUI, the initial authentication can be done via your central IdP (e.g., Microsoft Entra ID) using modern flows (SAML-based browser login), and then the Secure Login Client obtains a short-lived X.509 certificate to do SNC SSO to your ABAP systems. No Kerberos/KDC dependency required. 

Does SAML fit here (instead of Kerberos)?

 SAML (or OIDC) fits as the initial authentication step (IdP login), and the resulting outcome is still an X.509 cert used for SNC to SAP GUI systems. This is exactly how SAP positions it for “pure Entra / no on-prem KDC” situations. 

About the “virtual user” concept

In many modern Secure Login Service deployments, you no longer need the same “virtual user on AS Java Secure Login Server” construct, because the server-side enrollment is provided as a cloud service (no AS Java dependency) and the identity is driven by the IdP authentication + mapping. 

SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI:
https://www.sap.com/germany/products/financial-management/secure-login-service-for-gui.html (SAP)

Regards Colt

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP - Profit Center Description Not Appearing in...
entry.content: Follow‑on Update Follow‑on update on Profit Center Description not visible in Location‑Product MDT As a follow‑up to my earlier post, I ran Adapt Planning Objects for the last 30 days, aligned with the period during which the configuration changes were made. Despite this, the Profit Center Description is still not visible in the Location‑Product (compound) master data type / planning object view. What has already been validated: The Profit Center Description attribute exists and is active in the Profit Center MDT. The attribute has been added to the Location‑Product MDT and is active. Relevant Planning Area checks are clean, and Adapt Planning Objects completed successfully for the impacted timeframe. The expectation is that Profit Center Description should be derived via join/lookup from Profit Center MDT based on Profit Center code, not manually maintained at Location‑Product level. In some planning templates, the value appears for limited combinations only, suggesting a possible propagation or dependent table update issue, rather than a pure UI problem. Excel add‑in cache effects were ruled out by fully closing and reopening Excel sessions (not just refresh). Current gap: Even after Adapt Planning Objects execution, the Profit Center Description does not consistently appear in Location‑Product MDT or downstream planning views for all valid combinations. Looking for expert guidance on further checks, specifically: Whether additional steps are required beyond Adapt Planning Objects to propagate referenced MDT attributes to compound MDTs / lower planning objects. Any known prerequisites or constraints around exposing referenced attributes (like descriptions) in compound MDTs. Whether Planning Level or PA‑attribute exposure can block visibility even when MDT configuration is correct. If this behavior could indicate a known limitation or defect in IBP master data propagation. Any insights or recommended diagnostics would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Unable to save prompt templates in SAP AI Launchpad
entry.content: Seems like it is mandatory to have the "ailaunchpad_genai_manager" role collection, or the role genai_manager to be able to save prompts. The roles prompt_manager, prompt_ecperimenter and genai_experimenter are not sufficient to be able to save the templates created. This only allows you to save the prompt as a whole

entry.title: Re: Fiscal year 2026 is incorrect for post-capitalization...
entry.content: The closed fiscal year is 2024. So, you'd have to post the post-capitalization in the first open fiscal year, i.e. 2025.

entry.title: Re: Italy SDI B2G invoice send failing – cert status erro...
entry.content: Start by re-importing the full certificate chain into CPI’s keystore and double-checking the alias used in the “Italy Send Invoice” iFlow. If the chain is correct and the alias is valid, raise a ticket with SAP — they may need to provide updated trust certificates or a patch for the SDI adapter.

entry.title: Emarsys Forms questions
entry.content:
Hi,

I have some questions about Forms in Emarsys. Are the following things possible with them? 

1. Multi-lingual forms

I have noticed when you create a Form, that you need to choose 1 language. However, if you later add this Form into a Web Channel Campaign (Embedded or Overlay) and use a Content Block that allows to include a Form, I noticed it is possible to overwrite the labels that you have on the form. How far could you go with that? Could there somehow be variable labels added here that have a different value depending on the language of the website that this web channel campaign is added to?

2. Conditional fields

I have a single select dropdown field for "Type of Business". One of the options in this field is "Other". If the "Other" value is chosen, I would like an extra freetext field to appear where they can describe themselves their Other value. 

Or another example is if in the "Type of Business" list, the value "Restaurant" is chosen, that an extra field appears "how many seating spots do you have?".

Is this posible?

3. Hidden fields

Can you have hidden fields with a fixed value on a Form? For example the field "Country", that you could set to a fixed value "Belgium" in the background in a hidden field in the form, because this form would be deployed on a Belgian website.

Or, for a mini-form (with only an email address field) specifically to Subscribe to the newsletter, to have the Opt-in checkbox also selected & hidden on the background, as the form already specifies that it is specifically to subscribe.

4. Dynamic redirect url

You have a setting to which url the form needs to redirect after all the fields were correctly filled in and the form was submitted. This could be the url to a published brochure or leaflet. Do we need to create a separate form for each of our different brochures in each different language, or is there some way in which the redirect can be dynamic? i.e. the Website will have a page "download our Brochure A" where the form will be embedded, can it somehow then tell the form it should redirect to the download link for Brochure A, while on the website page to download Brochure B it can tell the same form to redirect to the download link for Brochure B?

entry.title: Re: yaai & yaai_ui – Open-Source AI Tools for ABAP Developers
entry.content:
Hi @Karthik-K26 ,
Thank you so much for your kind words and for taking the time to share your feedback. I’m really glad to hear that the blog was helpful and that everything worked well for you.

Regarding your question, this behavior is intentional. The function-calling output is meant to be consumed directly by the LLM, along with the previous messages and tool call history. Since the LLM processes it as part of the conversation context, it only needs a textual payload, which can be plain text, CSV, JSON, XML, or basically any format you want.

entry.title: Re: Display the attachedment directly in the web browser
entry.content:
Just copying the answer:

Yes, it’s possible, but it depends on the file type and server settings. Files like PDFs or images can open directly in the browser if the server sends them with the header Content-Disposition: inline instead of attachment. If your PR system enforces downloads for security, you’ll need to check with the admin or vendor to see if inline display is supported.

entry.title: Re: advanced variant configuration ETO public cloud
entry.content:
The issue isn’t with the code or URL, it’s with your data setup. The ETO profile is stricter than CTO — it requires a BOM view ID. Compare your custom BOM setup against the delivered scope item BOM and you’ll see the missing piece.

Next step

  • Assign a valid BOM view ID to your custom BOM.
  • If you only created a single-level BOM without specifying a view, add the “Production” or “Engineering” view.
  • Re-test the ETO profile. The error should disappear once the BOM view ID is available for binding.

entry.title: On the RPT-1 podcast: practical AI for SAP data (demos, lessons, and what’s next) 🎙️
entry.content:
If you work in SAP, you already know the truth: our world is rows, columns, master data, transactional history, and process context — not creative writing, in other words - SAP is not a “write me a poem” platform. That’s why I really enjoyed this conversation on SAP RPT-1, where we went beyond “AI hype” and into what’s genuinely useful for SAP practitioners.

RPT-1 is built for structured enterprise data. In plain terms: it’s designed to reason over the kinds of datasets we live in every day—think finance, supply chain, procurement, operations—where accuracy, traceability, and consistency matter more than fancy wording.

What you’ll get from the episode

  • A practical view of “AI for SAP data” — where it fits (and where it doesn’t)

  • Real demos and prototypes focused on outcomes, not theory

  • How to think about predictive + analytical use cases that start small but scale (without turning into a 6-month science project)

  • A clear message: move from chatbot experiments to measurable business impact

Why this matters for the SAP community

Most AI discussions ignore a key point: SAP value is locked in structured data + process discipline. The moment you align AI with that reality—tabular signals, business semantics, governance—you shift from “cool demo” to real operational leverage.

If you’re exploring AI for SAP and you want something that’s grounded in enterprise reality (data quality, governance, repeatability, outcomes), this episode is a solid watch:

🎥Watch here my podcast with Holger and Goran from Microsoft:
https://youtu.be/CbUDRgEO0yI?si=J4vE-BZ0Xig_srYo



Please share your thoughts and feedback! 

Cheers,
Amit Lal
